page: Amazon Web Services g7e.8xlarge is a graphics-intensive instance powered by the Nitro hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture with an Intel Xeon 8559C processor, providing 32 dedicated vCPUs across 16 physical cores operating at 2.4 GHz. The CPU includes a 320 MB L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) for tile and int8 operations. The instance is equipped with 256.0 GB of DDR5 memory running at 6 GHz, a single NVIDIA Blackwell RTX Pro 6000 GPU with 96 GB of VRAM, and 1900 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. Network connectivity is rated at a baseline of 100 Gbps.

Benchmark data indicates top-tier performance in LLM inference tasks. Prompt processing and text generation speeds across small (135M), medium (7B), and large (70B) models consistently rank in the top 10% of tested systems. Single-core performance is also highly competitive, with Geekbench single-core and image processing scores reaching top-tier status. Memory performance is strong, with uncached large-block read bandwidth measured at 198.11 GB/sec and a Passmark memory score in the top 25%. Multi-core CPU performance, ray tracing, and database operations fall within the average range.

Given the inclusion of a high-capacity 96 GB Blackwell GPU, DDR5 memory, and local NVMe storage, the g7e.8xlarge offers high resource density. It is qualitatively cost-efficient for workloads that require substantial VRAM and memory bandwidth without needing multi-GPU setups. This hardware profile is highly suited for large language model inference, image processing, graphics-intensive rendering, and high-throughput data decompression tasks.
description: Amazon Web Services g7e.8xlarge is a graphics-intensive instance featuring 32 dedicated vCPUs from an Intel Xeon 8559C processor, 256.0 GB of 6 GHz DDR5 memory, and a 1900 GB local NVMe SSD. It integrates a single NVIDIA Blackwell RTX Pro 6000 GPU with 96 GB of VRAM, supported by a 100 Gbps baseline network. Benchmark results highlight top-tier performance in LLM inference for prompt processing and text generation across small, medium, and large models, alongside strong single-core and image processing capabilities. The instance provides high resource density, making it a qualitatively cost-efficient option for workloads requiring massive VRAM and fast local storage without the overhead of multi-GPU configurations. It is highly suited for large language model deployment, graphics rendering, and high-throughput data processing.
og_description: Amazon Web Services g7e.8xlarge is a graphics-intensive instance featuring an Intel Xeon CPU, 256 GB DDR5 RAM, a 1900 GB NVMe SSD, and an NVIDIA Blackwell RTX Pro 6000 GPU with 96 GB VRAM for high-performance workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services g7e.8xlarge features 32 vCPUs, 256 GB DDR5 RAM, 1900 GB NVMe SSD, and an NVIDIA Blackwell RTX Pro 6000 GPU with 96 GB VRAM.
tagline: A high-density graphics and memory instance featuring a Blackwell GPU with 96 GB VRAM and fast local NVMe storage.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon 8559C processor with 32 dedicated vCPUs and AMX support
NVIDIA Blackwell RTX Pro 6000 GPU with 96 GB VRAM
256 GB of high-speed 6 GHz DDR5 memory
1900 GB of local NVMe SSD storage and 100 Gbps network bandwidth
Top-tier LLM inference performance for small, medium, and large models
Suited for large language model deployment and graphics-intensive rendering
